rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes several non-trivial inferential and design claims (Beta-Bernoulli conjugacy enabling closed-form posterior, mean-field SVI for bias parameters, non-parametric prior vs. Gaussian misspecification); logical consistency of these claims is a plausible load-bearing reading of 'check this.'
rationale.R1.3: Concrete quantitative assertions are present (38% rank-inversion reduction vs. Dubois et al. regression baseline, Spearman rho=0.91); verifying these align with the stated methodology is central if the check is technical in nature.
rationale.R1.6: A copy-editing or prose-polish reading of 'check this' is equally plausible given the vagueness of the delegation, making sentence clarity, terminology consistency, and paragraph-level quality potentially load-bearing.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I check this for technical and logical correctness (internal consistency of the model specification, inference procedure, and reported benchmark results), or for writing quality (clarity, grammar, and prose polish)?
